message:
Fix per-pane command guard pack override, case-insensitive snippet extraction, and mouse scroll panic

command_guard.rs:
- Pass PaneGuardConfig to keyword_filter() so per-pane enabled_packs
  overrides are respected during the keyword pre-filter phase. Previously
  keyword_filter() always used the global policy's enabled_packs, meaning
  a pane configured with only "core.git" would still match keywords from
  "core.filesystem" and other globally-enabled packs, causing incorrect
  blocking decisions. Now when a pane has an explicit enabled_packs list,
  only those packs participate in keyword matching.
- Add per_pane_enabled_packs_override test verifying that a pane with
  only core.git enabled allows rm -rf (filesystem pack not active for
  that pane) while still blocking git push --force (git pack active).

tantivy_query.rs:
- Replace manual to_lowercase() substring search with
  regex::RegexBuilder case_insensitive(true) for snippet term matching.
  The previous approach used text.to_lowercase().find(term_lower) which
  could produce incorrect byte offsets when the original text contains
  multi-byte characters whose lowercase form has different byte length
  (e.g., Turkish İ → i̇ changes byte count). The regex approach matches
  against the original text directly, producing correct byte positions.
- Use the regex match length (m.end() - m.start()) instead of term.len()
  for highlight marker placement, since case-insensitive matching may
  find a span with different byte length than the search term.

mouseevent.rs:
- Replace two unwrap() calls on self.terminal_size.rows.try_into() with
  unwrap_or(isize::MAX), preventing a panic if the terminal row count
  cannot be converted to isize. This is defensive — the value is
  typically small — but the unwrap was in a scroll event handler where a
  panic would crash the GUI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
